goal: Scale the clean MMS forced-alignment content path beyond the 40-row pilot and compare against the tuned chunked baseline.
reason: The scaled alignment data increases text diversity, but neither the broad 250-row set nor the correct-only set beats the tuned chunked baseline on the strict text-held-out comparison.
interpretation: Batch romanization made scaled content-corpus generation practical on Windows.
MMS forced alignment and Arabic time projection now scale cleanly: both scaled250 and correct200 had exact projection for all aligned rows.
The broad scaled250 set added much more variety but likely included noisy learner transcripts, which hurt recognition.
The correct-only set was cleaner and performed better than broad scaled250, but still did not beat the tuned baseline.
The next content step should not simply add more rows; it should use curriculum/sampling so new diverse aligned chunks do not overpower the stable original chunk distribution.
next_step: Train a curriculum model: start from the tuned HD96 baseline, mix original chunks heavily with a small amount of correct aligned chunks, and gradually increase aligned-chunk exposure only if strict text-held-out accuracy improves.
